LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT JIN FAN HE'S DEMURRER TO THE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT. DEFENDANT HE'S DEMURRER IS OVERRULED AS TO ALL CAUSES OF ACTION EXCEPT THE 11TH CAUSE OF ACTION FOR DECLARATORY RELIEF. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: THE DEMURRER IS SUSTAINED AS TO DEFENDANT HE WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THE 11TH CAUSE OF ACTION, DECLARATORY RELIEF, UNDER CCP 1061. THAT CAUSE OF ACTION IS UNNECESSARY BECAUSE THE BREACH OF CONTRACT CAUSE OF ACTION WILL RESOLVE THE ISSUES IDENTIFIED IN THE DECLARATORY RELIEF CAUSE OF ACTION. DEFENDANT IS GRANTED 10 DAYS TO FILE AND SERVE AN ANSWER. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. (302/CWW) JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR 5797

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Compelling Deft Jin Fan He To Attend Deposition And For Sanctions
Off Calendar Sep-28-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearance:
Anood Taqui, Esq., for Plaintiff and moving party: Lily Chan

No other appearance, tentative ruling is adopted. Plaintiff Lily Chan's Motion for order compelling defendant Jin Fan He to attend deposition and for sanctions granted:
1. Within 10 days of notice of entry of this order, defendant Jin Fan He shall appear, and provide testimony, at deposition;
2. The date, time and place of the deposition shall be determined by plaintiff, or as otherwise agreed by the parties;
3. Request for sanctions granted; sanctions shall issue in the sum of $1,780.00 for defendant's willful failure to comply with the court's previous order compelling the subject deposition. Order signed in open court. Commissioner: Paul Slavit. Not reported.

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Compelling To Attend Deposition And For Sanctions
Off Calendar Aug-10-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearance:
Anood Taqui, Esq., for Plaintiff and moving party: Lily Chan

No other appearance, tentative ruling is adopted. Plaintiff's Motion For Order Compelling Defendant Jin Fan He To Attend Deposition And For Sanctions Continued from July 21, 2009
Motion for order compelling defendant Jin Fan He to attend deposition and for sanctions granted:
1. Within 20 days of notice of entry of this order, defendant Jin Fan He shall appear, and provide testimony, at deposition;
2. The date, time and place of the deposition shall be determined by plaintiff, or as otherwise agreed by the parties;
3. Request for sanctions denied, without prejudice to further request should defendant fail to comply with this order.
Order signed in open court. Commissioner: Paul Slavit. Not reported.

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Recuse Pltf'S Attorney Due To Conflict Of Interest;
Off Calendar Aug-04-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT JIN FAN HE Motion to Recuse Plaintiff's Attorney Due To Conflict Of Interest. Appearances: Anood Taqui, Esq. Law Offices of Thomas Eastridge, (510) 530-1700, appearing for Plaintiff and opposing party. The Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Motion to recuse is denied. No evidence that Eastridge is currently representing defendant He. No evidence that a substantial relationship exists between the prior case and the instant case. The prior case was landlord/tenant and the instant case is construction defect. Request for stay is denied. Order signed in open Court.(302/CWW)Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard; Court Reporter: Kent Gubbine CSR 5797

Demurrer By Jin Fan He To The First Amended Complaint; Memorandum Of P/A
Off Calendar Jan-30-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT JIN FAN HE's Demurrer To The First Amended Complaint. Defendant He's demurrer is overruled as to all causes of action except the 11th cause of action for declaratory relief. Appearances: Anood Taqui, Esq., of the law Offices of Thomas Eastridge, appearing for Plaintiff and opposing party; There are no other appearances. The Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: The demurrer is sustained as to defendant He without leave to amend as to the 11th Cause of action, Declaratory relief, under CCP 1061. That cause of action is unnecessary because the Breach of Contract cause of action will resolve the issues identified in the declaratory relief cause of action. Defendant is granted 10 days to file and serve an answer. Order signed in Open Court. (302/CWW/PB) Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard, Reporter: Kent Gubbine, CSR 5797

MEDIATION ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE

Notice Of Motion For Order Compelling Deft To Attend Deposition And For Sanctions
Off Calendar Nov-05-2008 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearance:
Anood Taqui, Esq., for Plaintiff and moving party: Lily Chan
Chiu-Fan Ho, defendant and opposing party in Propria Persona

After hearing, tentative ruling is adopted with modification. Plaintiff Lily Chan's motion for order compelling defendant Steven Chiu-Fan Ho to attend deposition and for sanctions granted:
1. Defendant Steven Chiu-Fan Ho shall appear for, and submit to deposition within twenty days of the hearing at a date, time and place to be designated by plaintiff, or as otherwise mutually agreed by the parties.
2. Request for sanctions granted; sanctions shall issue in the sum of $1,140.00, payable within THIRTY days of the hearing.
MODIFICATION. 30 days for compliance and payment of sanctions. Commissioner: Paul Slavit, not reported.

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Compelling Deft Steven Chiu-Fan Ho To Answer Interrogatories, Respond To Request For Documents And For A Deemed Admitted Order
Off Calendar Nov-03-2008 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearance:
Thomas Eastridge, Esq., for Plaintiff and moving party: Lily Chan
Steven Chiu-Fan, defendant and opposing party, in Propria Persona
After hearing, tentative ruling is adopted with modifications. Plaintiff Lily Chan's Motion For Order Compelling Defendant Steven Chiu-Fan Ho To Answer Interrogatories, Respond To Request For Documents, And For Deemed Admitted Order, granted in part, denied in part, as follows:
1. Except for attorney-client and attorney work product privileges, all objections to the subject discovery are waived;
2. All responses ordered herein shall be verified, and provided within 20 days of the date of hearing;
3. As to plaintiff's form interrogatories, defendant HO shall provide full, complete and verified responses within ten days of the date of hearing;
4. As to plaintiff's special interrogatories:
a. Defendant HO shall provide full, complete and verified responses within ten days of the date of hearing to special interrogatories nos. 1 through 20, inclusive; and 28 through 96, inclusive;
b. As to special interrogatories nos. 21 through 27, inclusive, motion denied;
5. As to plaintiff's request for production of documents, defendant HO shall provide full, complete and verified responses within 20 days of the date of hearing;
6. As to plaintiff's requests for admission:
a. Requests for admissions nos.1 through 48, inclusive, 52, 53, and 57 through 59, inclusive are deemed admitted;
b. Requests for admissions nos. 49, 50, 51, 54, 55, and 56 are denied.
Sanctions issued in the amount of $1,140 payable within 20 days. Order signed in open court. Commissioner: Paul Slavit, not reported.
